cases.response: Cool the burn with running water for 20 minutes. Remove tight items. Cover with sterile dressing.
Call emergency services immediately. Cover burn with clean cloth. Do not apply creams.
Apply firm pressure with a clean cloth. Elevate the injured area. Seek medical help if bleeding continues.
Apply direct pressure. Cover with sterile bandage. Seek urgent medical care.
Give 5 back blows then 5 abdominal thrusts. Repeat until the object is removed.
Give 5 back blows then 5 chest thrusts for infants.
Lay the person down and raise their legs. Loosen tight clothing and check breathing.
Call emergency immediately. Help the person sit calmly. Give aspirin if available.
Call emergency services immediately. Note the time symptoms started.
Turn off the power source. Do not touch the victim until electricity is off.
Keep the victim calm. Immobilize the limb. Seek medical help immediately.
Wash wound with soap and water. Apply antiseptic and seek medical care.
Sit upright and lean forward. Pinch the nose for 10 minutes.
Immobilize the injured limb. Apply ice and seek medical care.
Rest the joint. Apply ice, compression, and elevation.
Call poison control immediately. Do not induce vomiting unless instructed.
Drink fluids to prevent dehydration and rest.
Remove allergen if possible. Give antihistamine if available.
Use epinephrine auto-injector immediately and call emergency services.
Move to a cool place. Remove excess clothing and cool the body.
Rest in a cool area and drink water slowly.
Move to warm place and warm the person gradually.
Remove person from water safely. Check breathing and start CPR if needed.
Protect the head. Do not restrain the person. Turn to side after seizure.
Help the person sit upright and use inhaler if available.
Give sugary food or drink if the person is conscious.
Encourage hydration and seek medical help.
Avoid rubbing the eye. Rinse with clean water if chemical exposure.
Flush the area with running water for at least 20 minutes.
Remove stinger if present and apply cold pack.
Remove the stinger and apply cold compress.
Rinse with seawater and remove tentacles carefully.
Apply cold pack and monitor symptoms.
Allow rest and monitor for confusion or vomiting.
Keep head and neck stable and call emergency services.
Place tooth in milk and seek dental care immediately.
Cover lightly with sterile dressing and seek medical care.
Remove visible debris carefully and bandage wound.
Encourage small sips of water and rest.
Drink oral rehydration fluids to prevent dehydration.
Drink water or electrolyte solution.
Rest and drink fluids. Use fever medicine if needed.
Cool the skin and apply soothing lotion.
Do not pop the blister. Cover with sterile bandage.
Lay the person down, elevate legs and keep warm.
Encourage slow breathing and reassure the person.
Encourage slow deep breathing.
Remove excess clothing and seek medical advice.
Lay the person on the left side and ensure fresh air.
